mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS remains in the sub-1% regime.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.11% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1% are not statistically significantly higher than when FEDFUNDS >= 1% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 7.6% provides baseline for deterioration detection
regime_hint: labor market stress or recession onset
failure_condition: When UNRATE increases > 0.3pp month-over-month, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% MoM AND HOUST declines > 2% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 421147M and HOUST at 853K provide baseline for decline detection
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% MoM, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 98.0 signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate margin compression and reduced corporate profitability in a supply-constrained environment.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 98.6709 is near the trigger threshold
failure_condition: When INDPRO < 98.0, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating to below 50,000 jobs per month signals labor market momentum loss that precedes earnings downgrades. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days specifically during late-cycle expansions when employment growth has previously exceeded 150,000 jobs per month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 50,000 jobs after prior 3-month average > 150,000
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 135637K provides baseline for growth calculation
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes with prior strong employment, PAYEMS growth < 50K produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the regime-conditional coefficient differs insignificantly from the unconditional coefficient (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) and declining consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.2%) signals monetary accommodation combined with disinflationary pressure. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real rates compress and equity risk premiums decline.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.11% and CPIAUCSL at 231.485 support trigger evaluation
failure_condition: When both FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL MoM < 0.2%, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating to below 1% annualized signals late-cycle or recessionary demand weakness. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days specifically when GDPC1 growth has previously exceeded 2% annualized, indicating a sharp demand reversal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 1% after prior quarter > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 13746.2B provides baseline for growth calculation
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth decelerates from > 2% to < 1%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the regime-conditional coefficient is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining month-over-month signals synchronized supply and labor contraction indicating recession risk. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors immediately reprice recession probabilities upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM AND PAYEMS declines MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 98.6709 and PAYEMS at 135637K provide baseline for decline detection
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline MoM, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
